title: SLIP StreamDecoder has no maximum buffer size, enabling memory exhaustion
description:
File: python/src/lichen/slip/codec.py
Line: 116 (and class design)

The StreamDecoder class accumulates bytes in self._buf without any limit. If a malicious or faulty peer sends a continuous stream of bytes without END delimiters, the buffer will grow indefinitely until memory is exhausted.

```python
else:
    self._buf.append(byte)  # No size check
```

Attack scenario:
1. Attacker opens serial/USB connection to gateway
2. Attacker sends megabytes of non-END bytes
3. Gateway OOMs and crashes

IPv6 MTU is typically 1280-1500 bytes. A SLIP implementation should reject packets exceeding a reasonable maximum (e.g., 2048 bytes to allow for escape expansion).

Fix: Add a max_size parameter to __init__ and check before appending:
```python
if len(self._buf) >= self._max_size:
    self._buf.clear()  # or raise, depending on policy
    continue
```
close_reason: Fixed: Reordered overflow check to come before max_size check in feed() loop. The triggering byte is now properly skipped when overflow mode is activated, and the overflow flag correctly persists until END is seen.
